description: Hantavirus serotype reference data for clinical context.

serotypes.name: Andes virus
serotypes.notes: Only hantavirus with confirmed human-to-human transmission. Close contact required.

serotypes.name: Sin Nombre virus
serotypes.notes: Most common cause of HPS in North America. First identified 1993 in Four Corners region.

serotypes.name: Puumala virus
serotypes.syndrome: Hemorrhagic fever with renal syndrome (HFRS), nephropathia epidemica
serotypes.notes: Most common hantavirus in Europe. Biennial peaks correlate with beech mast years and bank vole population cycles.

serotypes.name: Hantaan virus
serotypes.notes: Prototype hantavirus. First isolated during Korean War (1976). Causes classical HFRS.

serotypes.name: Dobrava-Belgrade virus
serotypes.notes: Most severe European hantavirus. Multiple genotypes with varying virulence.

serotypes.name: Seoul virus
serotypes.syndrome: Hemorrhagic fever with renal syndrome (HFRS), mild
serotypes.reservoir: Rattus norvegicus (brown rat), Rattus rattus (black rat)
serotypes.notes: Globally distributed via rat trade and urban rat populations. Generally milder HFRS.
